title: Profile Characters in Web Applications
text: Profile Characters in Web Applications In Web applications, the GOLD character is not recognized, so it is not possible to use the normal Uniface profile characters, such as GOLD * or GOLD ?. Instead, the characters must be preceded by a backslash, for example, Gib/* to retrieve all occurrences that start with Gib in the specified field. To retrieve a literal backslash, two backslashes must be used. For example, to retrieve an item with model number VTM/24 , you would need to enter: VTM//24 It is possible to change this behavior by setting the $SWAP_GOLD_PROFILES assignment setting, so that the literal character, such as the asterisk ( *), is treated as a profile character. For more information, see $SWAP_GOLD_PROFILES . Related Links Retrieve Profile Characters A retrieve profile is used to control the scope of the data to be retrieved from the database. It consists of one or more values for one or more fields in an entity, often containing profile characters. In essence, a retrieve profile is a search query. Gold and Super Key Combinations Gold and Super key combinations are defined with ^GOLD_BASE_SET. By default, these key combinations are available in all keyboard translation tables in the USYS library that support a Gold key. Subfield Separator and Profile Characters Uniface uses part of the reserved area of each meta character set (that is, the first 32 characters plus the last character) to store characters with special meaning within Uniface, such as subfield separators and profile characters.
